core_mechanism: a heterogeneous-agent stochastic pairwise-exchange automaton (kinetic-exchange dynamics) in which a conserved quantity is repeatedly redistributed through random binary interactions, with each agent's transfer governed by a fixed nonlinear q-deformed (nonextensive-entropy) response parameter, driving the ensemble toward emergent steady-state redistribution patterns
brief_reason: The abstract introduces an automaton whose operators, each permanently tagged with the nonextensive entropic index q, repeatedly exchange values in probabilistic transactions and produce different long-term patterns of average asset redistribution, i.e. a q-parametrized pairwise-exchange redistribution process.
